steps.title: Use the square area formula
steps.content: The area of a square is side length times side length. With side length $94$ centimeters, the area expression must be $94\cdot 94$.

steps.title: Recall the area formula for a square
steps.content: A square with side length s has area = s × s = s².

steps.title: Substitute the given side length
steps.content: The side length is 94 cm, so: Area = 94 × 94 cm²

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• $2\cdot 94$: this uses only two side lengths, not side times side. Eliminated.
  • $2\cdot 94\cdot 94$: this doubles the square's area. Eliminated.
  • $4\cdot 94$: this is the perimeter, not the area. Eliminated.
  • $94\cdot 94$: side times side, which is the correct area formula. That is the correct answer.
